properties.remark: THE SMITH SHOAL WEATHERFLOW STATION RECENTLY MEASURED A 38 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A 36 KNOT WIND GUST WAS MEASURED AT THE UPPER MATECUMBE WEATHERFLOW STATION.
A 34 KNOT WIND GUST WAS REPORTED AT THE ALIGATOR REEF WEATHERFLOW STATION.
A WEST-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 40 KTS...OR 46 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 243 PM EST.
A SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE ASOS STATION AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AT 440 PM EST.
A 3-SECOND WEST-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 44 KTS...OR 51 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE KEYS ENERGY BIG COPPITT POWER SUBSTATION AT 555 PM EST.

MULTIPLE REPORTS OF A WATERSPOUT WERE RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. IT OCCURRED OVER PINE CHANNEL BETWEEN LITTLE TORCH KEY AND BIG PINE KEY AT 901 AM EST ON FEBRUARY 25TH. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT ABLE TO BE OBSERVED DUE TO AN OBSTRUCTED VIEW OF THE HORIZON.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. IT OCCURRED NEAR BIG PINE KEY AT 901 AM EST ON FEBRUARY 25TH. IT LASTED ABOUT 3 MINUTES. SPRAY RING UNKNOWN.

properties.city: 5 S EAST END OF LONG KE
properties.remark: AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A NEARLY STATIONARY WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF DUCK KEY. WATERSPOUT LASTED A TOTAL OF 4 MINUTES AND OBSERVER COULD NOT SEE A SURFACE SPRAY RING.

MEMEBER OF THE PUBLIC AND AN ON DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ON THE GULF SIDE OF MARATHON, NEAR THE AIRPORT BUT ABOUT 1-2 MILES OFF SHORE. LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS MOVING SLOWLY NORTHWARD WITH NO SPRAY RING VISIBLE DUE TO TREES OBSCURING THE HORIZON. WATERPOUT LASTED 3 MINUTES.

A MEMEBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT JUST ONE MILE OFF SHORE OF MARATHON AIRPORT THAT LASTED 5 MINUTES. WATERSPOUT WAS MOVING SLOWLY EASTWARD AND ABOUT ONE HALF MILE FROM SHORE WHEN IT DISSIPATED AS REMNANT CIRCULATION COULD BE SEEN OVERHEAD.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 2 TO 3 MILES FROM DUCK KEY. SPOTTER SAID THAT A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AND MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY SOUTHEASTWARD. SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED A FAMILY OF SIX WATERSPOUTS ASSOCIATED WITH THE CLOUD LINE MOVING SOUTH OF THE MIDDLE KEYS.
A MARINER REPORTED MULTIPLE SHORT-LIVED WATERSPOUTS ASSOCIATED WITH A DISSIPATING AREA OF SHOWERS OFFSHORE SAND KEY LIGHT. DURATION UNKNOWN.

A DEPUTY SHERRIFF CALLED A REPORT INTO THE MONROE COUNTY SHERRIFF DISPATCHER FOR A LARGE WATERSPOUT LOCATED OCEANSIDE ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF RAMROD KEY MOVING SLOWLY EASTWARD. WATERSPOUT HAS A SPRAY RING AND DEPUTY SAID IT LASTED 8 TO 10 MINUTES.

MONROE COUNTY DEPUTY SHERRIFF DEPUTIES REPORTED A WATERSPOUT WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY AND JUST SOUTH OF LITTLE PALM ISLAND MOVING EASTWARD. WATERSPOUT GREW IN SIZE FOR FIVE MINUTES WHILE STAYING JUST OFFSHORE BEFORE DIMINISHING. WATERSPOUT LASTED A TOTAL OF 20 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 40 MPH...35 KNOTS FROM THE NORTH NORTHEAST WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION LOCATED AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT BAYSIDE OF KEY COLONY BEACH. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED MORE THAN HALFWAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED, AS OBSERVERS VANTAGE POINT WAS OCEAN SIDE OF KEY COLONY BEACH ACROSS A CANAL. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ONGOING AT THE TIME OF THE CALL, DURATION UNKNOWN.

properties.city: 10 S WEST END OF LONG K
properties.remark: A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED NUMEROUS WATERSPOUTS SOUTH OF DUCK KEY OVER THE PAST 45 MINUTES TO AN HOUR. TWO WERE CURRENTLY IN PROGRESS, ONE WIDE WITH THE FUNNEL CLOUD REACHING NEARLY HALFWAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. THE OTHER FUNNEL CLOUD WAS SKINNY AND NOT AS FAR DOWN. A THIRD FUNNEL CLOUD WAS ALSO NOTED FURTHER WEST, SOUTH OF MARATHON. THIS ONE WAS ALSO SKINNY AND NOT FAR DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. IT WAS LOCATED ABOUT 5 MILES SE OF ISLAMORADA. BASED ON THE TIME OF THE PICTURES, THE WATERSPOUT FORMED AROUND 715 PM EDT ON MARCH 20TH. THE TIME OF DISSIPATION IS UNKNOWN, BUT LIKELY OCCURRED AROUND 740 PM. SPRAY RING UNKNOWN.

MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED SEVERAL WATERSPOUTS ALONG A CLOUD LINE OVER HAWK CHANNEL FROM SOUTHWEST THROUGH SOUTHEAST OF MARATHON OCCURRING BETWEEN 7 PM AND 730 PM. VISIBLE FUNNELS EXTENDED ONE THIRD TO HALFWAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE. ANY POSSIBLE SPRAY RINGS UNABLE TO BE OBSERVED DUE TO OBSTRUCTION BLOCKING VIEW OF OCEAN SURFACE.

MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS ALONG A CLOUD LINE NEAR THE REEF TO THE EAST OF LOWER MATECUMBE KEY. VISIBLE FUNNELS EXTENDED ONE THIRD TO HALFWAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE. NO SPRAY RINGS OBSERVED.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 603 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KTS...OR 45 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS RECORDED BY THE SMITH SHOAL LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION AT 708 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED BY THE CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION AT 1029 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KTS...OR 48 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS RECORDED BY THE CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION AT 1034 PM EDT.
A VESSEL REPORTED WATERSPOUT OFF THE COASTLINE OF BOCA CHICA. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.

PUBLIC SUBMITTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA A PICTURE WITH TWO WATERSPOUTS WITH SPRAY RINGS OFFSHORE WEST OF KEY WEST ON THE REEF. TIME AND LOCATION IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS OBTAINED, THIS WILL BE UPDATED.

A THIN WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED OFF OF PLANTATION KEY VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED APPROXIMATELY ONE THIRD OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS GENERATED A WIND GUST OF 60 KNOTS AS IT MOVED EASTWARD ACROSS CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT.
A SPOTTER ON DUCK KEY REPORTED 4 TO 5 WATER SPOUTS 2 TO 3 MILES OFFSHORE OF THE MIDDLE KEYS ON THE BAY SIDE. HAVE BEEN OBSERVED FOR PAST 15 MINUTES ALREADY.

MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED IN THE BAY SIDE WATERS ABOUT THREE MILES OFFSHORE OF MARATHON AIRPORT. DISTINCT SPRAY RINGS WERE OBSERVED WITH THESE THIN, NEARLY STATIONARY WATERSPOUTS THAT HAD BEEN OBSERVED FOR AROUND TWENTY MINUTES ALREADY.

NAS KEY WEST PERSONNEL OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES EAST OF BOCA CHICA. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE FROM THE CLOUD BASE. THE FUNNEL CLOUD WAS WIDE AT THE CLOUD BASE AND VERY THIN TOWARDS THE END. DURATION APPROXIMATELY 6 MINUTES.

NAS KEY WEST PERSONNEL OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES EAST OF BOCA CHICA. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE FROM THE CLOUD BASE. THE FUNNEL CLOUD WAS WIDE AT THE CLOUD BASE AND VERY THIN TOWARDS THE END. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A THUNDERSTORM PULSED AND DELIVERED A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS OR 43 MPH AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT.
A BAND OF SHOWERS AND EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORMS TRIGGERED AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY WHICH DELIVERED A WIND GUST OF 40 MPH OR 35 KNOTS AT CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT

A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY AN OFF DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST LOCATED ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTH SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE DUE TO OBSCURED HORIZON. WATERSPOUT LASTED LESS THAN FIVE MINUTES AND IT DISSIPATED AT THE TIME OF REPORTING.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED AROUND 10 MILES SE OF KEY COLONY BEACH VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED AROUND 730 PM AND LASTED ABOUT 15 MINUTES. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.
AN OFF DUTY NAVY OBSERVER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF ISLAMORADA, ON THE ATLANTIC SIDE. HE OBSERVED A SPRAY RING AND THE DURATION WAS BETWEEN 5 AND 10 MINUTES.
PUBLIC REPORTED WATER SPOUT APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE BAHIA HONDA BRIDGE. THERE WAS A VISIBILE SPRAY RING. WATER SPOUT WAS STILL VISIBILE AT TIME OF REPORT.

AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 8 MILES NORTH OF THE BIG COPPITT KEY. THERE WAS A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. WATERSPOUT WAS MOVING SLOWLY WESTWARD AND DISSIPATED AFTER 20 MINUTES.

AN NWS EMPLOYEE ON DUTY OBSERVED A THIN WATERSPOUT AT 220 PM EDT LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 11 MILES NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO HAVE LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.

A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OF MEDIUM WIDTH AT THE CLOUD BASE AND THIN FROM THE MIDDLE DOWN TO THE SURFACE. IT WAS TILTED ABOUT 45 DEGREES AND LASTED APPROXIMATELY 15 MINUTES. A SPRAY RING WAS ALSO VISIBLE.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. BASED ON RADAR DATA, THE WATERSPOUT LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.

AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A WELL-DEFINED WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 15 MILES SSE OF SUGARLOAF KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS UPRIGHT AND A DISTINCT SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. THE WATERSPOUT LASTED FOR AT LEAST 10 MINUTES.
NWS EMPLOYEES ON DUTY OBSERVED TWO THIN WATERSPOUTS APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE WITH THESE WATERSPOUTS. THE WATERSPOUTS LASTED APPROXIMATELY TEN MINUTES.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A SKINNY WATERSPOUT NORTHWEST OF THE CUDJOE KEY BLIMP FACILITY. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED APPROXIMATELY ONE QUARTER OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON AND A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES.
WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS MEASURED AT THE CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION.
WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS, OR 43 MPH, MEASURED AT THE CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION.

MULTIPLE REPORTS RECEIVED OF A WIDE, MATURE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF KEY HAVEN. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. IT WAS FIRST OBSERVED AT 234 PM AND DISSIPATED AT 241 PM.

A THIN WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED FROM NWS KEY WEST WEBCAMS NORTHEAST OF KEY WEST. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED A QUARTER OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE, BUT VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES.

NUMEROUS REPORTS OF A WIDE, MATURE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF EAST ROCKLAND KEY. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE EAST. THE SPRAY RING ACCELERATED EASTWARD, STRETCHING THE WATERSPOUT TO A 45 DEGREE ANGLE BEFORE IT DISSIPATED AT 422 PM EDT.

NUMEROUS REPORTS OF A WIDE, MATURE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF EAST ROCKLAND KEY. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE EAST. THE SPRAY RING ACCELERATED EASTWARD, STRETCHING THE WATERSPOUT TO A 45 DEGREE ANGLE BEFORE IT DISSIPATED AT 422 PM EDT. AN ADDITIONAL WATERSPOUT WAS ALSO VISIBLE IN PICTURES AND VIDEOS, WITH THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDING ONLY A FIFTH OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE.

MULTIPLE REPORTS RECEIVED VIA PHONE AND SOCIAL MEDIA OF A WATERSPOUT OVER KEMP CHANNEL. THE WATERSPOUT WAS UPRIGHT AND A DISTINCT SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. THE WATERSPOUT LASTED FOR AT LEAST 15 MINUTES.
NWS EMPLOYEE AND SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTED TWIN WATERSPOUTS LOCATED IN THE UPPER SUGARLOAF SOUND. THE WATERSPOUTS FORMED AROUND 650 PM AND LASTED ABOUT 10 MINUTES. SPRAY RING UNKNOWN.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT EAST OF ISLAMORADA. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A NORTH-NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE CWOP SITE IN CUDJOE KEY AT 643 PM.

MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS WERE REPORTED ABOUT 8 MILES WEST OF KEY WEST VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. PHOTOS INDICATE ONE WATERSPOUT WAS UPRIGHT AND QUITE WIDE ALL THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. THE OTHER WATERSPOUT WAS TILTED AND WAS MUCH MORE NARROW. A DISTINCT SPRAY RING WAS VISIBILE WITH BOTH WATERSPOUTS. THE WIDER WATERSPOUT LASTED AT LEAST 15 MINUTES, WHILE THE NARROWER WATERSPOUT LASTED ABOUT 10 MINUTES.
NWS EMPLOYEE, SOCIAL MEDIA AND KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT A FEW MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED AROUND 255 PM AND LASTED ABOUT 15 MINUTES. SPRAY RING VISIBLE.
